steps.title: Identify the behavioral signal in Walsh's finding
steps.content: Walsh documented that social platforms make users mindful of being perceived, which then changes how they manage their listening. The signal to identify is a direct admission from a user that others watching influences what songs they choose.

steps.title: Match the signal to the correct quote
steps.content: Choice A is the only quote showing this: the user picks songs based on what friends following them might like rather than personal preference. B, C, and D address algorithmic recommendations, shared listening experiences, and passive vs. active consumption—none demonstrate perception shaping choices. That matches choice A — A is correct.
